insights.headline: Light pest-pressure signals to monitor
insights.whatWeSaw: Visible pest-pressure signals on some foliage (chewing, stippling, or residue).
insights.whyItMatters: Catching pest pressure early keeps it from spreading across the planting.
insights.wavesAction: Treated the affected foliage today and built follow-up monitoring into the plan.
insights.nextVisitPlan: Recheck the affected foliage next visit to confirm the signals are easing.

insights.headline: Water coverage in one area is worth a check
insights.whatWeSaw: Visible dry-stress signals in one shrub area even though the property got rain this week.
insights.whyItMatters: That pattern usually points to coverage in that specific bed rather than the whole property needing more water.
insights.wavesAction: Flagged the area and will recheck coverage there next visit.
insights.customerAction: Check that the irrigation reaches that bed evenly rather than watering the whole property more.

insights.headline: We looked into what you flagged
insights.whatWeSaw: You mentioned: “Customer asked about yellowing on the gardenia hedge near the lanai.”. We checked it during the visit.
insights.whyItMatters: We want what you noticed tracked on the report, not lost.
insights.wavesAction: Noted it on this visit and built any follow-up into the plan.
insights.nextVisitPlan: Follow up on it next visit.

reportV2.photoSummary: Shrub beds show mostly healthy new growth. Scattered pest-pressure signals on the gardenia hedge along the south side. A few croton leaves show early leaf-spot signals. Beds near the driveway look slightly dry.
reportV2.smsSummary: Your tree & shrub report is ready: healthy — monitoring light pest pressure. No action needed on your end.

petAdvisory: Keep pets off treated beds and foliage until dry.
